entry.title: Re: Missing Email Notification Option for Requester in “M...
entry.content:
Good day @arnap_silea16cc 

Requester email notifications are not controlled by one generic workflow setting. Notifications for Emails are based on workflow scenario and the system (SAP) defines which use cases and which recipients are available in the respective workflow app.

For purchase requisition, purchase contracts and supplier invoices, the system can notify the workflow initiator or requester about the approval result because the recipients are predefined.

See the following references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/2c6e00fd27944d7aa6a2ff1231a76d29.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/a8bbfa839c394505a7557f8171637d1a.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/eaea55938df044a8a0beeb97ed6ac8cd.html?locale=en-US

Therefore, if your workflow configuration does not offer the requester or document creator as a recipient you can select, that is system driven and you cannot manually add the requester as an additional recipient.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Rebase, Merge, Repeat: The Power of the Parallel Line
entry.content:
Hello @FabianAckermann 

Thank you for the excellent breakdown of the Parallel Line lifecycle. We have just successfully merged a country rollout project into our Main Line.

Now that the project is live, we are debating whether to keep this parallel line as a 'permanent sandbox' for future exploration or delete it. In your experience with 3SL, is it more efficient to keep a branch active and continuously Rebase it to stay aligned with Production, or is it 'cleaner' to delete the line and create a fresh Branch only when a new project starts? I’m particularly concerned about the long-term effort of maintaining a 'stale' branch vs. the benefit of having an always-available testing environment.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Custom fields for Purchase Order - Not updating in Fiori
entry.content:
For anyone in future that might face similiar problem.

At the end I was able to achieve the correct update of values by using both Adaptation project of standard Fiori application and by extenting standard ODATA metadata with annotation.

I chosed extension of standard ODATA as adaptation project would resulted in multiple Fiori tiles for same application that would cause business confusion. To achieve update of one custom field one another one is filled what I did was:

In SAP class CL_MM_PUR_PO_MAINT_V2_MPC_EXT method "Define" at the end of method we added following coding enhancement to append annotation to ODATA service metadata:

ENHANCEMENT 1  .
    DATA lo_vann_target TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_mgw_vocan_ann_target.
    DATA lo_vannotation TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_mgw_vocan_annotation.
    DATA lo_vcollection TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_mgw_vocan_collection.
    DATA lo_vfunction   TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_mgw_vocan_function.
    DATA lo_vfun_param  TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_mgw_vocan_fun_param.
    DATA lo_vproperty   TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_mgw_vocan_property.
    DATA lo_vrecord     TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_mgw_vocan_record.
    DATA lo_vreference  TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_mgw_vocan_reference.

    TRY.

        lo_vreference = vocab_anno_model->create_vocabulary_reference( iv_vocab_id = '/IWBEP/VOC_COMMON' iv_vocab_version = '0001').
        lo_vreference->create_include( iv_namespace = 'com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1' ).
        lo_vreference = vocab_anno_model->create_vocabulary_reference( iv_vocab_id = '/IWBEP/VOC_CORE' iv_vocab_version = '0001').
        lo_vreference->create_include( iv_namespace = 'Org.OData.Core.V1' ).
        lo_vann_target = vocab_anno_model->create_annotations_target( 'MM_PUR_PO_MAINT_V2_SRV.C_PurchaseOrderItemTPType' ).

        lo_vannotation = lo_vann_target->create_annotation( iv_term = 'com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1.SideEffects' iv_qualifier = 'xQEExMM_REK_SIDE_EFFECT' ) ##NO_TEXT .

        lo_vrecord     = lo_vannotation->create_record( )  ##NO_TEXT.
        lo_vproperty   = lo_vrecord->create_property( 'SourceProperties' )   ##NO_TEXT.
        lo_vcollection = lo_vproperty->create_collection( ).
        lo_vcollection->create_simple_value( )->set_property_path( 'ZX_SOURCE_PROPERTY' )  ##NO_TEXT .

        lo_vproperty   = lo_vrecord->create_property( 'TargetProperties' )   ##NO_TEXT.
        lo_vcollection = lo_vproperty->create_collection( ).
        lo_vcollection->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'ZX_TARGET_PROPERTY1' ) ##NO_TEXT.
        lo_vcollection->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'ZX_TARGET_PROPERTY2' ) ##NO_TEXT.

      CATCH cx_root.

    ENDTRY.

ENDENHANCEMENT.

 

As displayed in example we actually update values of 2 other custom properties not just one.

Same annotations could be use in adaptation project via VS code.

NOTE: Be aware that testing the changes is challenging as there are lot of metadata cache buffers between SAP system and loaded Fiori application in browser. It took some time for changes to be applied in frontend. I am not sure which exact caches must be cleared to make sure changes are effective in app.

Cheers

entry.content:
@Harsh_s1506  If you set in the subject still in the create_document and additonaly via the method it should work. Did this right minutes ago on a ECC 6.0. EHP 7 System . 

DATA(lo_document_ref) = cl_document_bcs=>create_document(
                                        i_type    = 'TXT'
                                        i_text    = lt_content_txt
*                                       i_subject = CONV so_obj_des( lv_subject ) ).
                                        
        lo_document_ref->set_message_subject( ip_subject = <long sunbject> ).

You see in the SOST the short version and the reciever see the long text.

entry.title: Why profile health is a non-negotiable part of your digital sales and marketing strategy
entry.content:
Most successful companies have a digital sales and marketing strategy. Whether customers learn about your brand through your website, industry marketplaces, or professional networks like LinkedIn, every channel plays an important role in how buyers perceive your business. 

For supply-side organizations selling through SAP Business Network, the company profile is one of those channels. It’s not merely an administrative page – it’s a buyer-facing storefront that significantly influences purchasing decisions. 

Treating your SAP Business Network account as a sales and marketing channel, and maintaining strong profile health, guarantees buyers see the best and most accurate version of your company.  


What profile health means on SAP Business Network 

Profile health is how complete, accurate, and optimized your company profile is on the network. It reflects how well you position your business for buyers as they search, evaluate suppliers, and make purchasing decisions on the platform.  
 
A healthy profile usually includes: 

  • Accurate business details (company name, addresses, website) 
  • Product and service descriptions 
  • Search keywords that help buyers find you 
  • Marketing materials and differentiators 
  • Updated contact information and geographic coverage 

In short, profile health shows how ready you are to help buyers discover and select your company. 

Research from McKinsey & Company highlights that B2B buyers regularly use 10 or more digital channels during their purchasing journey, and consistent information across those channels heavily influences how they pick suppliers.  

That means each channel, including your website, marketplaces, and networks like SAP Business Network, must tell the same story about your brand. 

Why profile health matters for your digital strategy 

Think of your SAP Business Network profile as another sales and marketing channel within your overall digital strategy. 

You wouldn’t launch a physical store without signage outside. You wouldn’t publish your website without adding where your products are available. And you wouldn’t leave anything on these channels outdated while investing in other marketing campaigns. But that’s exactly what happens when suppliers create a profile and never revisit it. 

Here are the main benefits of strong profile health on SAP Business Network:  

  • Increase discoverability 
    Buyers searching the network, including those that can now do this via their S4 applications, can easily identify suppliers who meet their needs. 

  • Strengthen brand consistency 
    Your profile reinforces the same messaging buyers see on your website, sales materials, and other digital channels. 

  • Speed up purchasing decisions 
    Buyers can immediately see whether you ship to their region, offer the right products, or meet sustainability expectations. 

  • Build credibility 
    A complete, well-maintained profile demonstrates a professional and reliable supplier. 

When information is missing, buyers often move on to the next option – include everything potential customers need to know about your business, so they don’t need to spend time browsing other channels.  


How to maintain strong profile health on SAP Business Network 

Like any sales or marketing channel, maintaining profile health requires ongoing attention and optimization.  

Keep your key company information up to date 

Constantly review important details such as: 

  • Company name and website 
  • Number of employees 
  • Business locations and shipping regions 
  • Legal structure and business type 

These let buyers quickly understand who you are and whether they can work with you.  
 
Optimize your business for search with keywords 

Buyers typically search SAP Business Network by product category or capability. And there’s around 40,000 of those buyer searches happening every six months, so adding relevant keywords for your business helps ensure your company appears in those searches. 

Some suppliers also use advanced tools, including AI-powered recommendations, to identify the best-performing keywords for their profile. These are available via a promote subscription for all SAP Business Network accounts.  

Highlight your industry expertise 

Your profile should also highlight what makes your business unique. Consider including: 

  • Sustainability initiatives 
  • Industry certifications 
  • Product one-pagers and case studies (available with a promote subscription) 
  • Thought-leadership content (available with a promote subscription)

These materials give buyers a sense of your value beyond basic product listings. 

Treat your profile like a living sales asset 

Successful suppliers who win repeat business do not create a profile and then forget about it. Instead, they continuously review and refine them, as they would any other digital channel. 

That might include: 

  • Testing different branding variations, such as a company logo 
  • Updating keywords as markets evolve 
  • Revising marketing materials 
  • Aligning messaging with marketing campaigns 

In a network of thousands of suppliers, small amendments can create a competitive advantage. 


Getting started: Updating your Company Profile  

Start improving your profile health on SAP Business Network by following these steps:  

  1. Log in to the supplier portal 

  2. Navigate to Company Profile from the drop-down menu under your initials 

  3. Select Company Profile 

  4. Update your business and marketing information 

  5. Configure keywords to improve discoverability 

  6. Check out if you’re ready for a promote add-on subscription by clicking on Store in the main navigation bar  


A strong profile is a stronger brand 

Maintaining a healthy profile on SAP Business Network is not about completing a form. It involves using the network as a part of your digital marketing and sales strategy. 

Suppliers who manage every channel by looking into the finer details, be it web, social media, B2B marketplaces, or procurement networks, tend to be the ones who stand out to buyers and win more business.  

Check out resources in the Community for more ways to get more from your SAP Business Network account.

entry.title: Re: How to check status of all configuration items (SSCUI...
entry.content:
Hello Ayukat,

Thank you for your question.

If your requirement is to export SSCUI configuration status specifically for Excel analysis from SAP S/4HANA Cloud, then yes, this can be done using the standard Export to Spreadsheet functionality. However, it is important to highlight that there is no standard way or delivered report in the system that provides a consolidated view of all SSCUI statuses (Open, Confirmed, etc.) in a single screen.

To meet this requirement, the possible approach is to use the Export functionality and analyze the data in Excel.

The typical solution involves the following steps.
1) Open the Solution Configuration – Change Phase app.
2) Make sure that all configuration items are visible:
3) Remove any filters and load the complete list of SSCUIs.
4) Personalize the table to include relevant columns such as:
SSCUI ID
Configuration Item Name
Status (Open / Confirmed)
Scope Item
Business Area (if available)
5) Click on the Export button in the top-right corner of the table.
Choose Export to Spreadsheet.
The system will download an Excel file (.xlsx) containing all visible configuration items and their statuses.
After exporting, you can structure the data in Excel for reporting.


With this approach, you can easily monitor:
Which SSCUIs are completed (Confirmed)
Which are still pending (Open)
Overall configuration completion progress

Since there is no standard consolidated reporting option available in the system, this Excel-based approach is the most commonly used and practical workaround in projects.

 

Best regards
HM Awasthi

entry.title: Driving Efficiency with SAP S/4HANA 2025 Reactive Maintenance(4HH)
entry.content:
The Reactive Maintenance Process Flow (4HH)

  1. Initiation & Screening: A notification is created for a breakdown, then screened/accepted.
  2. Planning & Approval: The request is converted into a maintenance order, with resources and parts planned and approved.
  3. Preparation & Scheduling: Parts are ordered, and the work is scheduled.
  4. Execution & Completion: Work is performed, confirmed via Fiori apps, and technically completed. 

2025 Key Updates & Best Practices

  • Best Practice 4HH: SAP strongly recommends adopting the new 4HH scope item, which has replaced older best practices (BH1, BH2, BJ2).
  • "Completed in Addition" Status: When operating in resource scheduling (e.g., using the "Manage Schedule" app), maintenance jobs that are added after the schedule is frozen are tracked as "Completed in Addition."
  • Emergency Order Handling: The "Notification Processing Context" field identifies emergency work, allowing for immediate assignment to technicians.
  • Mobile Capability: Maintenance requests and actions can be executed via mobile apps

    Phases and Subphases

System : S4HANA 2025

Script : Reactive Maintenance (4HH)

Configure Flexible Workflow for Maintenance Orders

Step 1. Log on : Login with Business Role Configuration Expert 

Step 2. Access the App : Fiori App—Manage Workflows for Maintenance Orders—Asset Management


Step 3. Check Automatic Approval for Maintenance Order


Create Planning Bucket

Step 1. Log On Log in with Business Role Maintenance Planner

Step 2: Access the App; Fiori App – Manage Maintenance Planning Buckets Old Version (F3888)



Step 3. Create New Planning Bucket




Create Maintenance Request 

In the initiation phase, an employee can create a maintenance request.

Step 1. Log On: Log in with the business role employee. 

Step 2: Access the App; Fiori App – Create Maintenance Request (F1511A)


Step 3. Enter Details

Step 4. Assess Priority



 

Step 4. Add Attachment/URL (Optional)

 

Step 5. Submit


Review Maintenance Requests 

Employee can check their existing maintenance requests.

Step 1. Log On: Login with the business role employee 


Step 2. Access the App

Fiori App - My Maintenance Requests (F4513)

Step 3. Search for Maintenance Request 

Step 4. Review Maintenance Request 


Screen Maintenance Requests

Screening phase of the process, the supervisor might need to review the maintenance request. 

Change responsibility of the requests and take appropriate decision to proceed with request or reject the request.

Step 1. Log On: Login with Business Role Maintenance Planner 


Step 2. Access the SAP Fiori app 

Fiori App- Screen Maintenance Requests(F4605)

Step 3. Get List of Maintenance Requests

Step 4. Change Responsibility


Step 5. Review and Modify the Maintenance Request



Step 6. Accept Maintenance Request


Create and Plan Maintenance Order

Once maintenance request is approved, planning phase starts. This process step shows how to convert a maintenance notification into a maintenance order and, provides details about planning the resources for the order.

Step 1. Log On: Login with Business Role Maintenance Planner 


Step 2. Access the App

Fiori App – Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders(F4604)

Step 3. Get List of Maintenance  Notifications

Step 4. Create or Assign Maintenance Order



Step 5. Plan Maintenance Order




Step 6. Define the Operations

 
 

Step 7. Add Stock and Non Stock Material




Step 8. Add Services

Step 9. Add Enhanced Limit Services

Step 10. Cost Review



Step 11. Check Entries

Step 12. Save Order

Submit Maintenance Order for Approval

Once we have planned resources for the maintenance order, it can be submitted for approval to the supervisor. This will set the status of the maintenance order to Ready to Prepare subphase.

Step 1. Log On: Login with Business Role Maintenance Planner 

 

Step 2. Access the App

Fiori App – Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders(F4604)

Step 3. Get List of Maintenance Orders

Step 4. Submit Order for Approval


Review and Release Maintenance Order

We are in Ready to Prepare subphase. In this process step, we can review a list of approved maintenance orders and release them for Preparation phase.

Step 1. Log On: Login with Business Role Maintenance Planner 

 

Step 2. Access the App

Fiori App – Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders(F4604)

Step 3. Review Maintenance Order

Step 4. Release Maintenance Order




Step 5. Review Purchase Requisitions

Maintenance Backlog Overview

Maintenance Backlog Overview screen will allow to view various cards. We can check Maintenance Backlog Card which shows backlog of maintenance orders based on phase or buckets.

Step 1. Log On:Login with Business Role Maintenance Planner 


Step 2. Access the App; Fiori App – Maintenance Backlog Overview(F5105)

Step 3. Open Maintenance Order via Maintenance Backlog Card

Step 4. Manage Maintenance Backlog Card

Step 5. Review Maintenance Orders and Maintenance Notifications

Step 6. Manage Maintenance Backlog Card (By Phase)


Step 6.1. Manage Maintenance Backlog Card (By Phase)


Step 6.2. Manage Maintenance Backlog Card (By Phase) -Keep Committed Quantities -Activate


Step 6.3. Manage Maintenance Backlog Card (By Phase) -Schedule Order


Step 7. Monitoring Procurement Activities

Convert Purchase Requisitions to Purchase Orders

In this process step shows how to assign purchase requisitions to a source of supply and to convert assigned source purchase requisitions to purchase orders.

Step1. Log On: Login with Business Role Purchaser 


Step 2. Access the SAP Fiori app

Fiori App- Process Purchase Requisitions (V2) (F1048)

Step 3. Search for Purchase Requisition

Step 4. Create the Purchase Orders

Goods Receipt for Purchase Order

Post the goods receipt for the purchase order.

Step 1. Log On: Login with Business Role Warehouse Clerk 

Step 2. Access the App

Fiori App- Post Goods Movement(F0843)

Step 3. Post Goods Receipt

Post Goods Issue

Execution phase of the process. Here you post the goods issue from storage location.

Step 1. Log On: Login with Business Role Warehouse Clerk 

Step 2. Access the App

Fiori App- Post Goods Movement

Step 3. Post Goods Issue

Submit Order for Scheduling

Once Preparation is done and backlog is reviewed, the order can be set for Ready to Schedule to start Scheduling phase.

Step 1. Log On : Login with Business Role Maintenance Planner 


Step 2. Access the App

Fiori App- Find Maintenance Orders(F2175)

Step 3. Get List of Maintenance Orders

As Order is already in scheduling phase so we do not have to execute this step further

Schedule Maintenance Order and Submit for Execution (F2175)

In this step, we can schedule the order operations and set the operations ready for execution.

Step 1. Log On: Login with Business Role Maintenance Planner 


Step 2. Access the App

Fiori App- Find Maintenance Orders

Step 3. Review List of Maintenance Orders

Step 4. Set Orders to ready for Execution


Manage Output

In Execution phase, technician will execute pre and main operations.

Step 10. Time Confirmation

Complete Main Work

After execution of Pre and Main work by the Technician, the Maintenance Supervisor will review the work and change the status from Main Work Completed to Post Execution phase.

Step 1. Log On: Login with Business Role Maintenance Planner 

Step 2. Access the App

entry.title: No Assign Button for Operations in SSAM
entry.content:
Supervisors are unable to Assign Technicians to Operations from the Service and Asset Manager App (v2405) in our test environment because there is no Assign Button.  This was present (and worked) before but recently disappeared. 

We have enabled Supervisor mode, created Supervisor Role (Position) in HR Org structure and assigned to work center.  There are Transfer and Clock In buttons.  The transfer button allows supervisors to transfer operations to a different work center (because they are using WO Assignment Type = 6).

Any suggestions or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks,

Bob

entry.title: Re: DRC Restriction – Support Document (CS) with Inventor...
entry.content:
Hello @adrian321 

The Colombia Support Document is what SAP delivered for localisation process used when the supplier is not obliged to issue an electronic invoice and the buyer must submit the electronic support document to DIAN.

Find some details here: 3432505 - eDocument Colombia Cloud Troubleshooting Guide - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

For your specific requirement, I am not aware of KR or KG being used as a supported replacement for the support document process and still be accepted by Colombia DRC as a valid support documents for DIAN. SAP does provide configuration to activate source document types and assign eDocument types to accounting document types, but that alone does not prove that KR or KG are supported substitute for the delivered support document process. That also fits into the be behaviour you mentioned of the document being posted correctly in ERP but the DRC submission fails when the legal support document is performed.

See the following references for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cb9131e59ba34741923bd19ef4cb1f90/e3f47af8d5404a138005bf686387b5c7.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cb9131e59ba34741923bd19ef4cb1f90/556be5c8c27443599c3c0e9771118bcb.html?locale=en-US

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Architectural Option for RISE with SAP: Running SAP S/4HANA on IBM Power Virtual Server
entry.content:
Architectural Option for RISE with SAP: Running SAP S/4HANA on IBM Power Virtual Server

Organizations adopting RISE with SAP typically approach the initiative as more than a technical upgrade. For many enterprises, the transition to SAP S/4HANA involves modernizing complex SAP landscapes that have evolved over decades.

These environments often include:

  • extensive custom developments
  • tightly integrated SAP and non-SAP applications
  • mission-critical operational workloads
  • strict availability and performance requirements

As a result, infrastructure architecture decisions can significantly influence migration complexity, project timelines, and operational outcomes.

One infrastructure option available for RISE deployments is running SAP workloads on IBM Power Virtual Server, a cloud environment based on IBM Power Systems architecture.

This approach can provide architectural continuity for organizations already operating SAP workloads on IBM Power infrastructure while also offering an alternative deployment model for new cloud ERP environments.

Architectural continuity for existing SAP landscapes

Many enterprise SAP environments run on IBM Power infrastructure due to its performance and reliability characteristics for large enterprise workloads.

Historically, migrating these environments to public cloud platforms often required replatforming from Power architecture to x86 infrastructure. This additional step can introduce complexity, including:

  • application and database compatibility testing
  • system tuning for different processor architectures
  • additional migration validation cycles

IBM Power Virtual Server provides a cloud infrastructure that uses the same underlying processor architecture as on-premises Power environments. This allows organizations to migrate SAP landscapes—including application servers and databases such as:

  • SAP HANA
  • IBM Db2
  • Oracle DB
  • AnyDB

without requiring a change in processor architecture.

In practical terms, this means infrastructure migration can occur independently from application transformation, potentially simplifying certain aspects of the migration process.

Evaluating Power Virtual Server for new SAP cloud environments

Organizations currently running SAP workloads on x86 infrastructure may also consider Power Virtual Server as an alternative cloud deployment model.

From an architectural perspective, several characteristics can be relevant for SAP workloads:

  • enterprise-grade reliability and availability
  • performance characteristics optimized for database-intensive workloads
  • security architecture designed for regulated environments
  • predictable infrastructure scaling behavior
  • an additional hyperscaler option for multi-cloud strategies

These factors can make IBM Power Virtual Server relevant not only for existing IBM Power customers but also for organizations evaluating infrastructure options during SAP S/4HANA transformation planning.

Impact of infrastructure architecture on migration timelines

Migration timelines for SAP S/4HANA programs can vary widely depending on system complexity, data volume, and landscape structure. Industry surveys indicate that many S/4HANA transformations span approximately 18 months pending on the complexity and size of the landscape.

When migrating from on-premises Power environments to Power Virtual Server, the shared processor architecture and virtualization model can reduce certain migration activities compared with heterogeneous migrations.

Based on IBM internal migration observations, this architectural alignment may reduce migration timelines by approximately 15–25% compared with migrations that require a change in processor architecture.

Migration programs can also leverage tooling such as the IBM Transformation Suite for SAP Applications, which supports:

  • SAP landscape discovery and assessment
  • code analysis and remediation
  • data migration planning
  • testing and validation automation

Reliability and security considerations

SAP ERP systems often support core financial, manufacturing, and operational processes. When moving these systems to the cloud, infrastructure reliability and security become key design considerations.

IBM Power infrastructure incorporates redundancy and resilience features designed for enterprise workloads. According to the 2023 ITIC Global Server Reliability Report, 93% of surveyed enterprise executives rated IBM Power systems with 99.999999% or greater availability.

Security is also relevant for organizations operating in regulated industries. Public vulnerability records in the NIST vulnerability database indicate fewer reported CVEs for PowerVM compared with several other hypervisor platforms used in enterprise environments.

While infrastructure architecture is only one component of overall system security, it can contribute to an organization’s broader risk management strategy.

Scaling infrastructure for large SAP HANA environments

Memory capacity is an important factor for large SAP HANA deployments.

Many cloud platforms offer SAP-certified instance profiles that increase memory capacity in large increments once databases exceed several terabytes.

IBM Power Virtual Server provides relatively granular memory scaling, with increments averaging approximately 2 TiB for large SAP environments.

The platform supports:

  • Scale-up configurations up to 32 TiB of memory per node
  • Scale-out configurations up to 128 TiB across four nodes

This scaling model allows organizations to align infrastructure capacity more closely with workload requirements and avoid unnecessary overprovisioning.

Supporting SAP analytics modernization

Many enterprises continue to rely on SAP Business Warehouse for operational reporting and planning workloads.

As SAP evolves its analytics architecture toward SAP Business Data Cloud, organizations often adopt a gradual modernization strategy rather than replacing existing analytical systems immediately.

Power Virtual Server can serve as an infrastructure platform for SAP BW on SAP BDC environments that require close connectivity with SAP S/4HANA systems.

Integration with services such as SAP Datasphere enables hybrid data architectures that combine established SAP BW workloads with newer data platform capabilities.

This allows organizations to modernize analytics capabilities while maintaining compatibility with existing SAP BW/4HANA deployments.

Summary

Infrastructure architecture can play an important role in the success of SAP S/4HANA transformation programs.

Running RISE with SAP on IBM Power Virtual Server provides one possible deployment model that offers:

  • architectural continuity for existing Power-based SAP environments
  • potential reductions in migration complexity
  • high availability infrastructure for mission-critical workloads
  • scalable memory configurations for large SAP HANA systems
  • integration opportunities with AI and analytics platforms

For organizations planning their RISE with SAP journey, evaluating infrastructure architecture options early in the planning phase can help align migration strategy, operational requirements, and long-term platform goals.

entry.title: Understanding the different ways to initiate the internal hire process in Onboarding
entry.content:
Internal mobility is one of the most powerful tools that an organisation has to retain talent, support career growth, and fill roles efficiently. But in SAP SuccessFactors, “internal hire” can mean many different scenarios — and each scenario behaves differently across Recruiting, Onboarding, and Employee Central.

Here we (myself, @NathanWilkinson and @Angela_Romano) will walk through 9 internal hire scenarios including the advantages, impacts, and decision points.

Before diving into the scenarios, it’s important to align on a few assumptions:

  • You’re using SuccessFactors Recruiting (RCM) and Employee Central (EC).
  • Event Reason Derivation is active in EC.
  • When we say “onboarding process”, we’re referring to any onboarding or crossboarding variant — not a single, universal workflow.

To get started, let's look at the standard process for an external onboarding process and use this as a contrast to the internal process: 

The Standard External Hire

  1. Recruiting (RCM) initiates onboarding.
  2. Onboarding (ONB) collects data and tasks.
  3. Manage Pending Hires (MPH) receives the candidate. This can be processed automatically.
  4. Employee Central (EC) creates a future-dated employment record.

 

This is the smoothest, most automated path which comes as standard out of the box for external candidates. Below we will discuss the 9 internal hire scenarios:

 

1. Internal Hire in the Same Legal Entity (our baseline)

This is the most common scenario and comes as standard out of the box.

Key considerations include:

  • EC will insert a future-dated row that is visible on the EC profile with the correct permissions
  • Workflows only show changed fields, not the overall view
  • Recruitment may not have all the data needed to trigger EC rules depending on complexity
  • Many customers want to review job and compensation data before approval.

In this flow, onboarding is initiated from RCM, but MPH is not used. Once the EC change is saved or approved, event reason derivation kicks in and the onboarding process can begin.

However, in our experience sometimes customers want an alternative to this standard. Below find just one of the processes we have implemented as part of solutioning on projects:

 

2. Internal Hire in the Same Legal Entity Using MPH

This variation gives you more control of what data you see.

  • An eligibility rule prevents onboarding from triggering automatically.
  • The hire lands in Manage Pending Hires, where data can be reviewed and edited.
  • Once submitted, EC creates a future-dated row and triggers workflows.
  • Onboarding can still be initiated after EC approval.

This approach is ideal when you want a checkpoint before data hits EC.

Please see  @NathanWilkinson's blog on this topic and how to configure the steps: https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/utilising-the-define-eligibility-for-onboarding-initiation-rule/ba-p/14251572

This brings us to our third process: 

3. Internal Move Initiated Directly in Employee Central

Not all internal moves start in Recruiting and we need to be able to initiate Onboarding via EC.

For promotions or internal transfers initiated by HR:

  • The employee does not apply in RCM.
  • A workflow approves the EC change.
  • Once approved, an onboarding or crossboarding process can still be triggered.

 

This is useful for moves requiring new documents or compliance steps where you may not need a full data collection but you do require an onboardee to complete compliance information. In this case you could have a process variant with fewer steps and streamline the process. 

 

4. Country Transfer (Different Legal Entity), Not a Global Assignment

This scenario covers when an employee is transferring from one legal entity to another but not on a global assignment. They need to be rehired which ensures compliance and proper separation between employment records.

 

  • Onboarding triggers a rehire check.
  • The employee must have a termination date before the new hire date.
  • After “Rehire on New Employment,” the employee receives a legal entity transfer email.
  • They complete onboarding using the Employment profile switcher.
  • Please be aware that the employee will require ESS access to do this. 

Please see @NathanWilkinson’s blog post on this topic: https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/using-successfactors-onboarding-for-country-transfers-without-recruiting/ba-p/14315749

On the contrary, when you are using a Global Assignment from Recruiting, we are able to do the following: 

5. Global Assignment Initiated from Recruiting

When a global assignment is created via RCM:

  • The hire lands in MPH from Recruitment.
  • A global assignment record is added once approved.
  • Onboarding begins, but Final Review is not supported.
  • The employee completes onboarding using the profile switcher.

Alternatively, if you are triggering the Global Assignment directly within EC, we can do the following:

6. Global Assignment Initiated directly in EC

Many organisations have mobility teams that manage assignments outside of Recruiting so often this is processed directly in to Employee Central.

  • A global assignment is added directly in EC.
  • Once approved, onboarding begins.
  • Final Review is not currently supported.
  • The employee completes onboarding via the employment profile switcher.

 

Similar to global assignments are concurrent employments. We can manage these in two ways; either via RCM or directly in EC.

7. Concurrent Employment Initiated from Recruiting

Similar to global assignments:

 

  • The hire is marked as Concurrent Hire in RCM.
  • The candidate lands in Manage Pending Hires.
  • EC adds the concurrent employment once approved.
  • Onboarding begins, again without Final Review.

8. Concurrent Hire Initiated Without Recruiting

This mirrors scenario 7 but starts in EC.

 

 

  • The concurrent hire lands in MPH.
  • Once approved, EC adds the new employment.
  • Onboarding begins.
  • Final Review is not supported.

Lastly, often we want to initiate an onboarding for contingent workers who then become employees. We may want them to sign a document, complete a compliance process etc. Here is the process of how that works: 

9. Changing Employment Type (e.g. Contingent Worker → Employee)

EC does not allow flipping a contingent worker to an employee.

  • The worker must be terminated and rehired.
  • The process resembles a country transfer in that we rehire them
  • A termination date must exist before the new hire date.
  • Onboarding is completed via the profile switcher.
  • ESS access is required but does not affect licensing.

I hope this blog has been helpful in giving you an overview of all the internal hire scenarios we have in Onboarding. The key message to take away from this blog is that when you are building your internal hire processes - it is essential to think about the bigger picture of the process from start to finish including Recruiting, Onboarding and Employee Central.

entry.title: Automatic Batch Determination Using FEFO for Transfer Posting (Movement 311) in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Automatic Batch Determination Using FEFO for Transfer Posting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Introduction

  • In industries such as pharmaceuticals, food, chemicals, and FMCG, managing material shelf life is critical. Organizations must ensure that materials with the earliest expiration dates are consumed or transferred first to prevent wastage.
  • SAP provides a First Expiry First Out (FEFO) strategy through Batch Determination to automatically select batches based on their expiration dates.
  • This blog explains how to configure and use Automatic Batch Determination using FEFO for Transfer Posting (Movement Type 311) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Step 1 – Create Strategy Type

  • To enable batch determination for transfer posting, first create a Strategy Type for Inventory Management.

Menu Path

IMG  -> General Business Data ->  Batch Management -> Batch Determination -> Batch Determination for Inventory Management -> Define Inventory Management Strategy Types

Image 1-1

  • A new strategy type “ZB12” was created to enable FEFO-based batch determination.

       Image 1-2

Image 1-3

Step 2: Create Search Procedures

Image 2-1

  • A new search procedure “ZB0003” was created and the strategy type “ZB12” was assigned to it.

Image 2-2

Step 3- Allocate IM Search Procedure/Activate Check:

  • Assigned search procedure “ZB0003” to movement type 311.

Image 3-1

Step 4- Create Sort Sequence:

  • A new sort sequence “ZFEFO1” was created and the characteristic “LOBM_VFDAT” (Expiration Date) was assigned to ensure batches are sorted in ascending order based on the earliest expiry date, enabling the FEFO strategy.

Image 4-1

Image 4-2

Step 5- Create Class

  • Two classes were created: “ZBATCH_FEFO1” for assigning to the material in the classification view and “FEFOCLASS” for use in the batch search strategy.

Image 5-1: “ZBATCH_FEF0”

  • Assigned the class to material “FEFO2”.

Image 5-2:

Step 6: Manage Search Strategies – Inventory Management:

Image 6-1

  • In “Manage Search Strategies – Inventory Management”, the strategy was assigned for movement type 311 and class “FEFOCLASS” was maintained.

Image 6-2

Image 6-3

  • Assigned the sort sequence “ZFEFO1” to enable FEFO-based batch sorting.

Image 6-4

Step 7: Maintain Shelf Life Data in the Material Master

Scenario Testing:

Below is the screenshot of Stock Overview for the following:

Material: FEFO2

The stock overview displays batch-wise details including:

  • Production Date
  • SLED (Shelf Life Expiration Date)

This information helps the system determine the appropriate batch during FEFO-based batch determination, ensuring that batches with the earliest expiry date are selected first.

Image 8-1

Testing – Transfer of 10 Quantity

A stock transfer was executed:

  • Movement Type: 311
  • From Storage Location: 1001
  • To Storage Location: 1003
  • Quantity: 10

After clicking the Batch Determination icon:

  • The system automatically assigned the batch.
  • The batch selected was the one with the earliest expiry date, as per FEFO logic.
  • The expiry date was displayed during batch selection.

Image 8-2

Testing with Increased Quantity (20 Qty)

Further testing was performed by increasing the quantity to 20.

Since:

  • The first expiry batch “FEFO567” had only 10 quantity available,

After clicking the Batch Determination icon:

  • The system automatically allocated
  • 10 Qty from Batch FEFO567 (earliest expiry batch)
  • Remaining quantity from the next earliest expiry batch

This confirms that the system splits the quantity automatically based on FEFO logic.

Conclusion:

The FEFO concept is functioning as expected during stock transfer using movement type 311.

  • Batch determination correctly selects the batch with the earliest expiry date based on the FEFO principle.
  • Expired batches are not considered during batch determination when the remaining shelf life condition is maintained.
  • The system automatically splits quantities across multiple batches when the required quantity exceeds the available stock in the first batch.

entry.title: The Fabric of Data & AI: Meet the Executive Speakers
entry.content:
The Fabric of Data & AI: Meet the Executive Speakers 

As organizations move from AI experimentation to real-world deployment, many are discovering a critical challenge: AI moves fast, but without business context it cannot exercise good judgment. 

For years, data strategies focused on extracting data into centralized systems for analytics. But this often stripped away the relationships and meaning that make data useful for real business decisions. 

At the Fabric of Data & AI, industry leaders will explore how organizations are addressing this challenge by building a business data fabric—an architecture that preserves business context across data, processes, and systems. 

Across the event, three themes will guide the conversation: technology, process, and culture—the pillars required to scale AI successfully. 

On March 24, join SAP’s Irfan Khan, President & Chief Product Officer for Data & Analytics, alongside executives from leading organizations who are actively building the foundations for enterprise AI. 

Meet the Customer Executive Speakers 

Google 

Jannie Affeld 
VP, Finance Systems and ERP 

Jannie oversees a global engineering team that builds, implements, and integrates financial applications used by the Alphabet and Google Finance teams.  
 
Jannie started her career as an Information System Auditor at Arthur Andersen. Her subsequent career in IT spans across Oracle, HP, Dell, Electronic Arts, Cisco and Visa. In these roles, she led various IT initiatives including ERP implementation, Business Intelligence, Billing, Revenue and Rating Systems, Supply chain, CRM and IT operations. She has in-depth experience operating on a global scale to deliver complex solutions. 

Ericsson 

Malin Persson 
CIO & Head of Enterprise IT 

Malin Persson is an accomplished technology and transformation leader with more than 20 years of experience driving digital innovation, operational excellence, and large-scale change across global and national organizations.  

Since 2025, she has served as CIO & Head of Enterprise IT at Ericsson, where she leads the company’s IT strategy, digital infrastructure, and enterprise-wide transformation agenda. In this role, she shapes the future of Ericsson’s technology landscape while steering critical modernization programs and ensuring resilient, business critical operations.  

Mead Johnson Nutrition 

Ganesh Sivakumar 
Global Chief Data Analytics & AI Officer 

Ganesh Sivakumar is a globally recognized leader in enterprise AI transformation, with over two decades of experience driving large-scale digital innovation across Fortune enterprises. Currently serving as Global CDAIO at Mead Johnson Nutrition, Ganesh has delivered exceptional business value by embedding advanced analytics, machine learning, and generative AI into core business functions.  

Ganesh has led large-scale data, analytics, and digital platform transformations, embedding governance, data quality, and responsible innovation as core enablers of business value. His expertise lies in moving enterprise data and advanced technologies beyond fragmented initiatives into governed, scalable, and business-driven production ecosystems. 

Team Liquid 

Steve Arhancet 
Co-CEO & Co-Owner 

Steve Arhancet has helped define what it means to be a new media organization. Founded in 2000 and merged with Arhancet’s Curse Gaming in 2015, Liquid Enterprises today spans the breadth of content and events production,  influencer agency, product development and website management. On a day-to-day basis, Steve's priority is the development and performance of Team Liquid’s 160+ world-class athletes across 23 unique esports, as well as overseeing Team Liquid’s strategic direction.  

With over a decade of esports experience at every level, from pro player to founder, Arhancet is an industry expert and a frequent guest in media. On a day-to-day basis, Arhancet's priority is the development and performance of a stable of world class gamer athletes worldwide. 

Join the Conversation 

The shift from traditional analytics architectures to context-driven AI systems is one of the most important transformations happening in enterprise technology today. 

At the Fabric of Data & AI, you’ll hear directly from the leaders shaping that transition—sharing how organizations are connecting data, processes, and context to power reliable AI. 

📅 March 24, 2026 
🌍 Virtual event 

Register now and secure your spot: https://sap.to/6047hOkab

entry.title: Re: How to check status of all configuration items (SSCUI...
entry.content:
Good day @Ayukat 

Thank you for your question.

In the standard 2 system Solution Configuration, the status is at the item level in Configure Your Solution, where you have status values such as Open and Confirmed indicating which mandatory activities are still incomplete. You can also access these through full text search and selection fields.

I am not aware of any delivered report, export, or separate overview app that helps you consolidate all configuration item into one overall monitor with totals for confirmed versus op SSCUIs.

Also, View Solution Scope is mean to show scope information such as active business processes and country versions. It is not a status monitor for configuration item completion.

The simplest workaround is for you to search and filter items by status in Configure Your Solution.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Personas script - Prevent users to reach Main Screen
entry.content:
Hi,

Adding scripts to screen events can reduce the performance of the RFUI application on each screen update, so it is recommended to avoid this where possible.

You can try using the ~singletransaction=1 parameter, which can be added either to the URL or to the service-level parameters (in SICF). When used together with ~transaction=/SCWM/RFUI, it restricts access to the RFUI application only. When the user exits RFUI, they will be redirected to the Goodbye page.

Best regards!

entry.title: Re: Issue with Indirect PR Release Strategy & Workflow fo...
entry.content: Once cross verify the currency value AED was missing or not properly maintained in CT04 in QAS/PRD systems. After maintaining the correct value, the object was reassigned to the class using CL02 or CL30N to ensure proper classification. Once the changes were completed, a new Purchase Requisition was created and tested, and the release strategy was successfully determined and verified using ME54N.

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh + AMQP: The Retry Trap Nobody Warns You About
entry.content:
Re. how to retry from DMQ queue in Event Mesh.

There is no built-in option in Event Mesh to move messages between queues like there is for JMS, unfortunately.

You can create separate iFlow in CI that moves messages from DMQ back to the original queue, and then run it either on demand or automatically on a low-frequency schedule. This approach can also be used for JMS queues if manual intervention isn’t sufficient

br
Mateusz

entry.title: ME23N: PO Expected to Trigger Message Type NEUG but System Determines NEU.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am facing an issue in ME23N (Purchase Order display).

For a specific PO, the expected output type is NEUG, but the system is instead determining NEU.
I want to understand why NEUG is not getting picked and which logic/routine is overriding it.

I have already checked the basic setup:

  • Condition records exist for NEUG
  • NEUG is configured in NACE → EF (Purchasing)
  • Output type NEU also exists with the same access sequence
  • PO document type is correctly assigned

However, the system still picks NEU instead of NEUG.

Could you please guide me on what to debug and which objects or routines control the PO output determination?

Specifically:

  1. Where should I set breakpoints (FM or includes) to trace NEU/NEUG selection?
  2. Are there any exits/BAdIs (like MM06E005 or user routines in VOFM) that can override message determination?
  3. What should I check in the output access sequence or fields that could cause NEUG to be skipped?
  4. Any standard SAP recommendations for analyzing incorrect output type determination?

Any help or debugging steps would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Updating Backend-Generated Key in Offline Store after upload
entry.content:
MDK Version: 25.9
Platform: iOS

Problem
I'm creating notification entities offline with a temporary key. After uploading via upload action, the backend generates the real notification number, but the offline store still contains the temporary key, causing data inconsistency.

Current Flow
1. Create entity offline with temp key
2. Upload via ChangeSet - backend generates real key
3. Upload succeeds, but offline store retains temp key

Question
What is the recommended MDK pattern for updating the offline store with backend-generated keys after a successful ChangeSet upload? Should I use an UpdateEntity action post-upload, or is there a built-in mechanism?

entry.title: Re: SAP DM - how we empty the global variable
entry.content:
You delete a global variable from within the Production Process Designer (the app where you manage your production process design). Here’s how:

  1. In the design header section, click the number under Global Variable to open the global variable list.
  2. Locate the global variable you want to remove and initiate its deletion.
  3. If the global variable is used elsewhere in your production process, the system will automatically detect this and display a warning message that lists where it’s in use.
  4. At that point, you can review the dependencies (by clicking the Dependencies option in the Where-Used column) to see its usage details.
  5. Decide whether to cancel or continue with the deletion. If you continue, note that the related read/write global variables service in any production process using that global variable will show an error—and that service will no longer be usable.

entry.title: Re: SAP DM - how we empty the global variable
entry.content:
You delete a global variable from within the Production Process Designer (the app where you manage your production process design). Here’s how:

  1. In the design header section, click the number under Global Variable to open the global variable list.
  2. Locate the global variable you want to remove and initiate its deletion.
  3. If the global variable is used elsewhere in your production process, the system will automatically detect this and display a warning message that lists where it’s in use.
  4. At that point, you can review the Dependencies (by clicking the Dependencies option in the Where-Used column) to see its usage details.
  5. Decide whether to cancel or continue with the deletion. If you continue, note that the related read/write global variables service in any production process using that global variable will show an error—and that service will no longer be usable.

entry.title: Avoiding Double Counting of Yield/Scrap in Rework Scenario (SAP DM → S/4HANA Integration)
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am currently facing an issue with yield and scrap postings during a rework scenario between SAP Digital Manufacturing (DM) and SAP S/4HANA, and would appreciate your guidance.

Scenario:
1. At the first operation, 10 qty is completed → Yield = 10 is posted to S/4HANA.
2. Due to nonconformance, the SFCs are sent back to the same first operation (rework).
3. At the first operation:
・5 SFCs are scrapped
・5 SFCs are completed again

Observed behavior:
The second completion and scrap are posted additionally to S/4HANA.
As a result, the totals in S/4HANA become:
・Yield = 15
・Scrap = 5

However, the actual production result is:
・Yield = 5 (final good quantity after rework)
・Scrap = 5

So effectively, yield from the same operation is being counted twice.

Question:
What is the recommended approach in SAP DM–S/4HANA integration to prevent double counting of yield and scrap in such rework scenarios?

Specifically:
Is there a standard mechanism to ensure that confirmations from the same operation are not cumulatively posted in this way?

Should this be handled via configuration (e.g., rework routing design, reporting settings), or through enhancement (e.g., BAdI such as CO_MES_PRODORDCONF or similar)?

Are there best practices for handling rework loops to ensure correct yield/scrap reporting in S/4HANA?

Any guidance, configuration examples, or relevant SAP Notes would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: S/4Hana Cloud Public Edition OData Api Rate Limits
entry.content:
Dear @thomas_schmidt_valantic,

SAP does not appear to publish one universal numeric rate limit. Instead, SAP publishes API-specific limits/guidelines and technical constraints instead of one tenant-wide X requests/sec number for every OData API

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3391018

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3658248

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3626726

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3072317

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3622758

What SAP does publish is this:

  • For Journal Entry Post API, SAP gives performance recommendations for example no more than 15 parallel synchronous calls and 30 parallel asynchronous calls in Q and explicitly says these figures are guidelines only that may change depending on resource usage by other processes/tenants. SAP also warns that sending too many requests in a short period can get API access temporarily blocked and recommends retry handling with exponential backoff
  • For read scenarios, SAP documents paging / fetch limits per API for example:
    • Product APIs: default page size 100, maximum 5000 with $top, introduced to help prevent DOS-style overload
    • Purchase Order OData API: default page size 1000, maximum 5000 with $top
    • Custom OData APIs: maximum 5000 records per fetch, not increaseable
  • SAP also documents some general technical limits such as OData V4 URL length 8000 characters and max payload size 500 MB in Public Cloud

For architecture there is one more relevant SAP statement in the Business Partner mass-update context, SAP says OData is synchronous and batch updates are processed sequentially, not in parallel, which is another reason not to assume high-throughput bulk behavior from OData alone

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3542227

Summary:

SAP does not publicly define one fixed global rate limit for all Public Cloud OData APIs. Instead, SAP documents API-specific paging/performance limits and applies throttling guidance per API/scenario

IMPORTANT: If a hard numeric limit is required the right approach is to open an SAP incident for the specific API(s) and tenant context because SAP does not publicly publish one universal OData rate-limit value

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Receipt from Production: Unit Price Not Calculated from Actual Component Cost in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hello,

I am encountering a problem in SAP Business One during Receipt from Production.

Scenario:

  • I have a Production Order with actual component costs (Actual Item Component Cost).

  • When I post the Receipt from Production, the unit price of the finished product is not automatically calculated based on the Actual Item Component Cost.

  • This results in a variance between the component cost and the product cost.

Example:

  • Actual Item Component Cost = 1 000 000

  • Actual Product Cost = 1 200 000

  • Variance = 200 000

It seems that SAP is not automatically updating the product unit price from the actual component cost at the time of the receipt.

My questions:

  1. Is this expected behavior in SAP B1, or should the unit price always reflect the Actual Item Component Cost automatically?

  2. Is there a setting or configuration to ensure that the product unit price is calculated correctly from the actual component cost?

  3. Are there any best practices to avoid such variances or to handle this scenario correctly?

Thank you in advance for any guidance or insights.

entry.title: Re: Missing Assign Button in SSAM v2405
entry.content: The disappearance of the Assign button when WO_ASSIGNMENT_TYPE is set to 6 (Operation Work Center) usually points to a mismatch between the supervisor’s technical assignment and the specific Work Center of the Operation. Ensure that the supervisor is not only defined in the HR org structure but is also explicitly assigned as a Member or Head of the specific Work Center associated with that Operation in the backend (ECC/S/4HANA). Additionally, verify that the Mobile Status of the Work Order or Operation hasn't progressed to a state where assignments are locked (e.g., "Dispatched" or "In Review"), and double-check that the ASSIGNMENT_TYPE parameter hasn't been accidentally overridden in the specific Global Profile or User Parameter (SU3) settings for the testing environment.

entry.title: Re: Guidance Required for Defaulting GR Message Indicator...
entry.content:
Hi,

The GR Message Indicator is different from other fields such as “Goods Receipt Expected” or “GR-Based Invoice Verification.” While those fields are available in BAdIs or can be defaulted via master data (e.g., supplier or purchasing info record), the GR Message Indicator is currently not exposed in any Purchase Order BAdI or extensibility option.

Therefore, it is not possible to automatically default the GR Message Indicator for Purchase Orders created via batch jobs using standard in-app extensibility.

If you would like to request this functionality as a feature, we recommend submitting an idea through the SAP Customer Influence Portal, which is reviewed by Product Managers for potential inclusion in future releases. You can follow the steps in this KBA to submit your idea 

  1. Get started by opening SAP Customer Influence SAP Continuous Influence sessions for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 
  2. Register and accept the Terms of Use
  3. Choose a session of your interest
  4. Create and submit your improvement request
  5. Take a look at existing improvement requests and start voting and commenting

Thanks and regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price is not getting updated
entry.content:
Dear @Rajubothra,

Since the material is batch-managed and uses valuation category X, SAP automatically creates the valuation type during goods receipt. In this scenario the valuation type is system-generated and corresponds to the batch-specific valuation logic, so it is not necessary to create valuation types manually. In split valuation, SAP valuates the material at valuation-type / partial-stock level, not only at one generic material level. Therefore, after PO and GRN the relevant price update is expected on the specific valuation type created for that batch, while the generic/header material valuation record may not show the change in the way the business expects

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/f00478e53c224ded8c40255a65aefdf4.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4032610758dc437089f0c28320eec93f/f15fac0ceaba41d1a78ce9230b9f8bc8.html

Please also note that the result depends on the price control used for that valuation record:

  • if the valuation type uses moving average price the price can be updated by goods movements
  • if it uses standard price the stock quantity and stock value are updated but the standard price itself is not automatically changed by GR

Summary:

So the correct place to verify the updated price is the valuation-type-specific / batch-specific valuation data, not only the generic material valuation view. SAP Help also confirms in stock/material document apps for split-valuated materials that you can work with or display the valuation type directly

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/6cb7d3af780a4cf2934f2bf485ab72b7.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7f47a6d9441c46ac86c96fd27f6015f0/bbd9b6ce0be14516ad7868da0982024a.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7f47a6d9441c46ac86c96fd27f6015f0/52db2a2dcc0146c2adb39b68fab8deda.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: PostgreSQL Schema Evolution in SAP CAP - How to Handl...
entry.content:
Thanks so much for the kind words.

Great question: in MTA, `@cds-pgroll/postgres` runs as a dedicated pre-deploy task/module, not a sidecar.
Flow is:

1) `pgroll-migrator` runs first (`node run-pgroll.js`)
2) `db-deployer` runs after it (`cds-deploy --model-only`)
3) `srv` starts last

In `mta.yaml`, this is enforced with `deployed-after` on the db-deployer.

So you keep a clean CI/CD pipeline with no manual SQL steps.

entry.title: Exploring Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1 (HFC6)
entry.content:
I'm excited to share the latest highlights for Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1 (HFC6). This release brings significant enhancements to Manufacturing and Production Planning, designed to provide production planners, supervisors, and supply chain managers with greater control, visibility, and efficiency. Lets explore the new capabilities that will help you streamline your operations.


Manage Material Coverage - Shipping Notification Quick View

In the Manage Material Coverage app (F0251A), we have introduced a new quick view for the MRP element Shipping Notification (ShpgNt). This enhancement provides planners with immediate access to critical information without navigating away from their main screen. The quick view displays details about the supplier, the responsible internal contact, and the confirmation status received from the supplier.

Manage Material Coverage - Shipping Notification Quick View

For deeper analysis, you can now navigate directly to the related purchase order from this view, significantly speeding up issue resolution and improving situational awareness.

Improved Stock Transfer Between MRP Areas

The overall stock transfer process between MRP areas is seen below. 

Stock Transfer Between MRP Areas

For what business use case do you need this new scenario?

The business case is that this new process allows you to have several storage location MRP areas (warehouses) now that are supplying your production warehouse.

Why do you need several source storage locations (warehouses)?

One of the reasons might be that you might want to have your supplier deliver raw materials or components to these source storage locations first, and then do a stock transfer from these source based locations to your production site or to your main warehouse in the production facility.  Meaning in SAP language that the raw materials or components that are stored in the source warehouses (source MRP area) are now needed in the target production facility (target MRP area). Also, another reason might be that you want to line up more than one source MRP area and then in the target MRP area you do the final assembly. 

Now with the latest release, the MRP can be used for the stock transfer of components from the warehouse (source MRP area) to the production facility (target MRP area) by either using a stock transfer reservation or requisition. 

Example: Stock transfer reservation

This feature improves the value of independent MRP areas and minimizes master data maintenance efforts, streamlining the planning process.


Capacity Scheduling Board

We have improved the user experience within the Capacity Scheduling Board. For better visibility and context during scheduling, you can now display the Product ID and Product Description directly in the Gantt chart. Simply access the Gantt Settings and select the Product ID and Product Description column to add it to your view:

View settings: Product ID and Product Description

 The result after selecting the above named settings looks like the following: 

Product ID and Product Description included as information

This removes the need to look up product details separately, making the scheduling process faster and more intuitive for your planners.

Manage Work Center Capacity

Another highlight is in the app Manage Work Center Capacity (F3289). Here we added the capability in the overview section to select the utilization with Maintenance as seen in the following screen: 

Utilization with Maintenance Settings

This gives the production planner not only the view of the utilization for planned or production orders, but also for maintenance orders to improve capacity planning on the respective Work Centers. 

Also, we included the maintenance order capacity information (in % and hours) in the table overview, so that the production planner can also use the grid view: 

Table with Maintenance Capacity View

 With this addition the maintenance order capacity value is now included in the utilization chart of the Manage Work Center Capacity Fiori app to give a holistic planning view to the production planner for their day to day work. 

SAP Cloud ERP: Manufacturing | Demos - How Tos 

Last but not least, I would like to introduce our new SAP Cloud ERP: Manufacturing  channel on YouTube to you. This new channel is embedded into the SAP Help and Learning YouTube channel which is your go-to destination for tutorials, product demos, and expert guidance on SAP solutions. Whether you’re getting started or looking to deepen your expertise, our videos cover setup, best practices, and step-by-step walkthroughs to help you succeed. If you have any topics where we need to create a Microlearning, please let us know. 

That's it and stay tuned - the next HFC is just around the corner. 

Best regards, 

Markus

entry.title: Re: Process Order BOM lists corrupted and cannot be corre...
entry.content:
From your post, it seems, you already checked with SAP who recommended a report and that did not solve your issue, my suggestion would be as follows:

  • Request SAP to validate whether Z_PPCON_124 handles scenarios where BOM components were changed after postings — it may not fully reset reservations if the BOM was incorrectly adjusted mid-process.
  • Ask if SAP has a standard correction program or OSS note for “negative withdrawn quantity” issues in process orders after BOM changes.
  • Provide technical details:
    • Process order numbers: 2502707799, 2502660794.
    • Current reservation status and component list mismatch.
    • Screenshots/logs showing negative withdrawal values.
  • Seek guidance on whether reservation re-creation (via CO02 or backend correction) is required instead of just running the correction report.
  • Confirm if SAP recommends a database-level adjustment or a specific note/patch to handle this scenario, since it seems beyond standard correction logic.

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price is not getting updated
entry.content:
Hi Raju,

If your business process requires the price to be "picked up" from the Purchase Order automatically, Price Control S is technically the wrong setting.

* With Control S: The system insists on a pre-defined price. It will never let the PO dictate the material value. 

You should consider using price control V instead.

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.title: Re: SAP DM - how we empty the global variable
entry.content:
Hi,

there are two straightforward ways to empty a Global Variable in SAP Digital Manufacturing:

Option 1: Script Task

Add a Script Task to your production process and assign an empty value matching the variable's data type:

  • String: Global.myVariable = "";
  • Number: Global.myVariable = 0;
  • Boolean: Global.myVariable = false;
  • Object / complex type: Global.myVariable = null;

Note: Global Variables do not support expressions directly — the reset must be done inside a Script Task, not in a condition expression field.

Option 2: Write Global Variable Service Step

In the Production Process Designer, add a Write Global Variable service step to your automation sequence and set the value to "" (empty string) or 0 depending on the variable's data type. No scripting needed.

A few things to keep in mind:

  • Global Variables persist across process instances and are not automatically cleared when a process completes — you need to explicitly reset them as shown above.
  • There is no "delete" button in the UI; the approaches above simply overwrite the value with an empty/default state.
  • If you want to avoid stale data, a good practice is to place the reset step as the first step in your process so it always starts clean.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price is not getting updated
entry.content: We would like to inform you that we have enabled valuation category X at the header level. We have also maintained the price at the header level and assigned price control S at the segment level. Since SAP supports price control S for actual costing, we have entered the price in the purchase order. However, during GRN, the system is removing the price from the material master at the header level. As the valuation category is X, the system automatically generates the batch after GRN. Due to this behavior, it is not possible to create the batch and maintain the price before GRN.

entry.title: Grouping of Reservations into Purchase Requisition based on Reservation Priority as additional param
entry.content:
Hi,

when I run MRP (md02, md01) in ECC, sap clubs multiple reservations into single purchase requisition because lot size procedure is EX and requirement date is identical for reservations. I have requirement to generate multiple purchase requisitions base on reservations’ priorities as additional parameter. for example,

all 10  reservations have requirement date march 14, 2026. 3 reservations have priority 1, 4 reservations have priority 2, 3 reservations have priority 3. so i want to generate 3 purchase requisitions.

can you advise which enhancement can help to handle this scenario?

thanks,

Sajjad
